title: Extracts of Red Wine and Grape Juice. In addition to measuring changes in the urine metabolome, the second group also identified 12 metabolites in human plasma that changed as a result of consuming a mixture of extracts of red wine and grape juice for 5 days. The authors described the statistically most interesting metabolite changes to be reductions in tyrosine at minus 8 percent, sphingomyelin at minus 2 percent, and dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate at minus 12 percent. In addition, levels of threonine, lysine, and two lysophosphatidylcholines decreased but by no more than 5 percent. The authors noted that levels of the plasma amino acid tyrosine decreased, while its corresponding gut microbial metabolite, p-cresol sulfate, also decreased in urine.

sentences.text: Extracts of Red Wine and Grape Juice.

In addition to measuring changes in the urine metabolome, the second group also identified 12 metabolites in human plasma that changed as a result of consuming a mixture of extracts of red wine and grape juice for 5 days.

The authors described the statistically most interesting metabolite changes to be reductions in tyrosine at minus 8 percent, sphingomyelin at minus 2 percent, and dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate at minus 12 percent.
In addition, levels of threonine, lysine, and two lysophosphatidylcholines decreased but by no more than 5 percent.
The authors noted that levels of the plasma amino acid tyrosine decreased, while its corresponding gut microbial metabolite, p-cresol sulfate, also decreased in urine.
